name: DBRX
archDisplay: Fine-grained MoE, 16 experts (4 active per token)
note: Fine-grained MoE (132B total/36B active) pretrained on 12T tokens; a leading open model at launch, beating GPT-3.5 on MMLU/GSM8K/HumanEval.
tpsNote: Exceeds a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM even at Q4 (~66GB needed); requires multi-GPU or CPU offload

results.notes: 25-shot, DBRX Instruct, from the official Databricks launch blog's open-model comparison table
"MT Bench (Inflection Corrected)", DBRX Instruct, reported alongside GPT-3.5/GPT-4/Claude comparison, score 8.39 ± 0.08

Big Bench Hard (leaderboard_bbh, acc_norm 0.541225) from HF Open LLM Leaderboard v2's independent lm-eval-harness run on databricks/dbrx-instruct, fetched directly from the raw results JSON. Not in the file's current 8 tracked results (which are all from the Databricks launch blog).

GPQA Diamond subtask (leaderboard_gpqa_diamond, acc_norm 0.348485) from HF Open LLM Leaderboard v2's independent run on databricks/dbrx-instruct. The leaderboard also reports a combined main+extended+diamond average (34.14%) but Diamond alone is used here to match this corpus's usual GPQA convention.
IFEval (leaderboard_ifeval, prompt_level_strict_acc 0.482440) from HF Open LLM Leaderboard v2's independent run on databricks/dbrx-instruct. Not previously tracked.
MMLU-Pro (leaderboard_mmlu_pro, acc 0.368268) from HF Open LLM Leaderboard v2's independent run on databricks/dbrx-instruct. Distinct from the already-tracked plain MMLU self-reported score (73.7).

verificationNotes: Fully confirmed via fetch of the official Databricks blog post: release date March 27, 2024; 132B total/36B active fine-grained MoE with 16 experts (4 active per token); 32K max context; 12T pretraining tokens. License name 'Databricks Open Model License' confirmed verbatim via databricks.com/legal/open-model-license. All four benchmark rows (MMLU 73.7, HumanEval 70.1, GSM8K 66.9 5-shot, HellaSwag 89.0) literally appear on the cited blog page. No changes made; nothing dropped.
